title: Unclear Onboarding Flow Post-Signup
description: Users are experiencing significant confusion immediately after signing up, struggling to identify the next steps and nearly abandoning the product within the first few minutes.
reasoning: This is a critical problem as it directly impacts new user retention and activation. If users cannot understand how to begin using the product, they will churn immediately, negating all efforts in user acquisition.

title: Mobile UI Elements Unresponsive
description: Users are experiencing significant usability issues on mobile devices, with critical UI elements like buttons being unresponsive and scrolling functionality being broken, making the product unusable on phones.
reasoning: This problem directly impacts core mobile functionality, preventing users from interacting with the application. It's a critical blocker for mobile users and severely degrades the product's accessibility and utility on mobile platforms.

title: Misleading Free Plan Billing
description: Users are inadvertently incurring charges after believing they are on a free plan, indicating a lack of clarity in pricing and billing communication, especially regarding plan transitions or hidden costs.
reasoning: Unexpected charges lead to significant user frustration, distrust, and potential churn. It directly impacts user perception of fairness and transparency, which are critical for long-term customer relationships.

title: Lack of Slack Integration for Notifications
description: Users are unable to receive important updates and notifications directly within Slack, forcing them to log into a separate tool to stay informed. This creates friction and reduces efficiency.
reasoning: Many users rely on Slack as their primary communication and notification hub. The absence of an integration means users miss timely updates or have to context-switch, leading to a poorer user experience and potential delays in action.

title: Email Notifications Not Sending
description: Users are not receiving email notifications for events they have configured, leading to a complete failure of the alert system for these users.
reasoning: This is a critical issue as users rely on notifications for timely updates and engagement. A complete failure to send notifications renders a core feature unusable and significantly degrades the user experience and product utility.

title: Notion Import Processing Stuck
description: The Notion import process gets stuck in a 'processing' state indefinitely, preventing users from successfully importing their data and making the feature unusable.
reasoning: This is a critical blocker as users cannot transfer their data from Notion, rendering a key integration feature completely broken and leading to significant user frustration and data loss concerns.

title: Bulk Actions Limited to Small Batches
description: The bulk actions feature restricts users to processing only 10 items at a time, making it inefficient and time-consuming for users with large datasets (e.g., 200+ entries) to perform necessary updates.
reasoning: This limitation significantly hinders productivity for users managing substantial amounts of data, forcing them into repetitive, manual processes that should be automated. It directly impacts efficiency and user satisfaction for power users.

title: Missing CSV Data Export Functionality
description: Users are unable to export their data in CSV format, which is a standard and expected feature for reporting and data analysis in other tools they use.
reasoning: The lack of CSV export is a significant blocker for users who rely on this functionality for essential weekly reporting, making the product less useful and potentially causing them to seek alternatives.

title: Search Functionality Fails to Return Relevant Results
description: The search functionality is completely broken, failing to return any results even when users input exact project names, forcing manual navigation and wasting significant user time.
reasoning: A non-functional search feature is a critical blocker for user productivity and core navigation, making the product difficult or impossible to use efficiently for finding specific items.

title: Mandatory Credit Card for Trial Access
description: Users are required to enter credit card details before they can access or even view the product's features, creating a barrier to initial exploration and trial engagement.
reasoning: This requirement creates friction at the very first step of the user journey, preventing potential users from experiencing the product's value proposition. It can lead to high bounce rates for users who are hesitant to provide payment information upfront without seeing what they are signing up for, thus hindering user acquisition and trial conversions.

title: Lack of Team Collaboration Features
description: Users are unable to invite teammates or share their work within the product, hindering collaborative workflows and centralizing all work to individual accounts.
reasoning: This is a critical blocker for any team-oriented use case, preventing users from leveraging the product for collaborative projects and limiting its utility to solo work. It directly impacts user adoption and retention for teams.

title: Dashboard Slow with Large Datasets
description: The dashboard experiences significant performance degradation and timeouts when users manage a large number of projects (e.g., over 50), rendering it unusable for teams with extensive project portfolios.
reasoning: This is a critical blocker for users with larger datasets, forcing them to abandon the product for core functionality. It directly impacts scalability and adoption for enterprise or power users.

title: API Documentation Outdated and Inaccurate
description: The API documentation is not kept up-to-date with the actual API, leading to incorrect endpoint information and response discrepancies for users.
reasoning: Inaccurate API documentation severely hinders developers' ability to integrate with the product, causing frustration, wasted time, and potential abandonment of the API. It directly impacts developer experience and adoption.

title: Lack of Keyboard Shortcuts
description: The product currently lacks comprehensive keyboard shortcuts, forcing power users to rely on mouse-only workflows. This significantly reduces efficiency and productivity for users who prefer or require keyboard navigation.
reasoning: Keyboard shortcuts are essential for power users and accessibility, directly impacting productivity and user experience. Their absence creates friction and slows down workflows for a significant segment of the user base, potentially leading to dissatisfaction and reduced engagement.

title: Editor Loses Work on Tab Switch
description: The editor fails to persist user input when switching between browser tabs, leading to data loss and requiring users to re-enter their work repeatedly.
reasoning: This is a critical problem as it directly leads to data loss, a highly frustrating experience that erodes user trust and productivity. Users expect their work to be saved automatically or at least retained when navigating away temporarily.

title: AI Suggestions Lack Depth
description: The AI's suggestions are too generic and obvious, failing to provide novel or insightful information that users find valuable or actionable.
reasoning: If the AI consistently provides generic advice, users will perceive it as unhelpful and unintelligent, leading to disengagement and a lack of trust in the product's core value proposition.

title: Frequent Random Logouts Disrupt Workflow
description: Users are being unexpectedly logged out of the application during active sessions, leading to loss of unsaved work and significant workflow disruption. This issue occurs multiple times a day for affected users.
reasoning: Random logouts are a critical usability issue that directly impacts user productivity and data integrity. Users lose trust in the application if their work is not reliably saved and their sessions are not stable, potentially leading to abandonment.

title: Inability to Combine Multiple Filters
description: Users are unable to apply multiple filtering criteria simultaneously (e.g., date range and tags), severely limiting their ability to refine search results and find specific content efficiently.
reasoning: This problem significantly hinders user productivity and data discoverability. Users expect to be able to narrow down information using various attributes, and the current limitation forces them into inefficient, sequential searches or manual scanning, leading to frustration and wasted time.

title: Lack of Undo/Redo Functionality
description: Users are unable to revert accidental actions, leading to data loss and significant frustration, particularly with destructive operations like deletion.
reasoning: The absence of an undo function for critical actions like project deletion directly results in loss of user work, causing severe frustration and making the product unreliable for serious use. This is a fundamental expectation in modern software.

title: Lack of Dark Mode Option
description: The product currently lacks a dark mode option, leading to eye strain and discomfort for users who work in low-light environments or prefer a darker interface.
reasoning: This is a significant problem because it impacts user comfort and accessibility, especially for those working extended hours or with light sensitivity, potentially leading to reduced usage or dissatisfaction.
